title_original: University of Glasgow spinout Quantcore raise £2.5m seed
summary_original: Quantcore, a University of Glasgow spinout, has secured £2.5 million in seed funding from PXN Ventures, Blackfinch Ventures, Scottish Enterprise, Quantum Exponential, and STAC to develop the United Kingdom’s sovereign supply chain for quantum hardware. Operating from the James Watt Nanofabrication Centre, Quantcore uniquely manufactures niobium-based quantum components, enabling higher operational temperatures and improved energy efficiency for clients including national laboratories. The company’s technology underpins both quantum computing and advanced sensors with applications in secure communications and medical imaging. Founded by Dr Jack Brennan, Dr Valentino Seferai, Wridhdhisom Karar, and Professor Martin Weides in 2025, Quantcore aims to triple its team within 18 months, aligning with the United Kingdom’s strategy to expand quantum capability amid increased national investment and global competition.
